# `liber:storage@1`

Generated reference for the `liber:storage` package (`storage.lsidl`). See [the LSIDL language](../../../LSIDL.md).

LiberSystem storage package - volume and file services.

## Imports

- `error` from [`liber:base@1`](/docs/gen/liber/base/v1.md)

## Types

### resource `file`

An opaque file object, transferred to a client as handle<file> (a capability to a shared buffer of the file's bytes).

An opaque kernel object, transferred as `handle<file>`.

### record `open-opts`

Options for opening a path on a volume.

| field | type | description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `path` | `string` |  |
| `write` | `bool` |  |
| `create` | `bool` |  |

### record `open-result`

The result of opening a file: a capability to the file's bytes (transferred out-of-band as handle<file>) plus the file's length, so the client knows how many bytes the shared buffer holds.

| field | type | description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `file` | `handle<file>` |  |
| `size` | `u64` |  |

### enum `file-type`

Whether a listed entry is a regular file or a subdirectory, so a listing can show the two apart.

| case | ordinal | description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `file` | 0 |  |
| `dir` | 1 |  |

### record `file-info`

One entry in a volume listing: its name, byte length, whether it is a regular file or a subdirectory (a directory reports a length of zero), and its last modification and creation times (u64 seconds since the Unix epoch, UTC; 0 when the backing filesystem does not carry them).

| field | type | description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `name` | `string` |  |
| `size` | `u64` |  |
| `type` | `file-type` |  |
| `mtime` | `u64` |  |
| `ctime` | `u64` |  |

### record `snapshot-info`

One named snapshot of a volume: its name and the generation it pinned, so a client can present an ordered, identifiable list of restore points.

| field | type | description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `name` | `string` |  |
| `generation` | `u64` |  |

### record `volume-status`

A volume's identity and health, for `lsvol` and the `volume` command: its label, pool size and free space (the filesystem's own numbers, unlike `capacity` which asks the disk), whether transparent compression is on for new writes, whether the mount is read-only (a snapshot mount, or degraded by on-disk damage), and the name of the filesystem serving the volume ("liberfs", "fat16", "exfat", "iso9660", "udf", "archive"). Foreign backends track no pool numbers and report zero bytes.

| field | type | description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `label` | `string` |  |
| `total-bytes` | `u64` |  |
| `free-bytes` | `u64` |  |
| `compression` | `bool` |  |
| `read-only` | `bool` |  |
| `filesystem` | `string` |  |

### record `fsck-report`

What a volume integrity check found: how many data blocks failed their checksum, and the paths of the damaged files - so the operator knows what to restore.

| field | type | description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `checksum-failures` | `u32` |  |
| `damaged` | `list<string>` |  |

## Interfaces

### interface `volume`

The Storage volume contract: resolve a path on a volume and hand back the file's bytes as a shared-buffer capability. `open` returns the capability out-of-band (handle<file>) alongside its length - the capability-return case that exercises the handle<T> wire transfer. `list` enumerates the directory named by a vol:// path (the volume root when the path has no subdirectory). `write` / `remove` make the volume writable: `write` creates-or-truncates a file from a zero-copy `buffer`, `remove` deletes one; `mkdir` / `rmdir` create and remove (empty) directories. A read-only volume rejects the mutating ops with `denied`. `snap-create` / `snap-list` / `snap-delete` manage named read-only snapshots, and `snap-open` resolves a path inside a snapshot to read an earlier state. `capacity` reports the size in bytes of the block device backing the volume (asked of the disk, not the filesystem), for the `lsblk` inventory. `status` reports the filesystem's own identity and health numbers (label, pool and free bytes, the compression switch, read-only); `set-compression` flips transparent compression for new writes; `fsck` verifies every live block against its checksum and names the damaged files; `restore` copies a file out of a named snapshot (or, with an empty name, the previous generation) over the live file - the recovery verb for what `fsck` named.

Request `[op u16][corr u32][args]`, reply `[corr u32][result]`.

| op | method | signature | description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 1 | `open` | `open(o: open-opts) -> result<open-result, error>` |  |
| 2 | `list` | `list(path: string) -> stream<file-info>` |  |
| 3 | `write` | `write(path: string, data: buffer) -> result<unit, error>` |  |
| 4 | `remove` | `remove(path: string) -> result<unit, error>` |  |
| 5 | `snap-create` | `snap-create(name: string) -> result<unit, error>` |  |
| 6 | `snap-list` | `snap-list() -> result<list<snapshot-info>, error>` |  |
| 7 | `snap-delete` | `snap-delete(name: string) -> result<unit, error>` |  |
| 8 | `snap-open` | `snap-open(snapshot: string, path: string) -> result<open-result, error>` |  |
| 9 | `mkdir` | `mkdir(path: string) -> result<unit, error>` |  |
| 10 | `rmdir` | `rmdir(path: string) -> result<unit, error>` |  |
| 11 | `capacity` | `capacity() -> result<u64, error>` |  |
| 12 | `status` | `status() -> result<volume-status, error>` |  |
| 13 | `set-compression` | `set-compression(enabled: bool) -> result<unit, error>` |  |
| 14 | `fsck` | `fsck() -> result<fsck-report, error>` |  |
| 15 | `restore` | `restore(path: string, snapshot: string) -> result<unit, error>` |  |
| 16 | `write-stream` | `write-stream(path: string, data: handle<channel>) -> result<unit, error>` | The streaming write form (a file's size is bounded by the filesystem, never by one transfer): the caller sends the file's bytes as plain messages on `data` and closes it to mark the end; the reply comes once the whole file is written. |
